---
title: "Program Evaluation"
---

::: {.topic-badges}
[Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} · **Methods & Study Design** track · ~1 hour, self-paced
:::

[← Back to the Methods & Study Design track](methods-study-design.qmd){.track-back}

*Speed the scaffolding of an evaluation — logic models, indicators, plans — with expert review.*

## What you'll be able to do

- Draft a logic model and indicators with AI
- Build an evaluation plan aligned to program goals
- Verify indicators are measurable and meaningful

## Overview

AI can turn a program description into a logic model, propose indicators, and outline an evaluation plan. You confirm the indicators are actually measurable, aligned to goals, and not just plausible-sounding.

Evaluation frameworks (e.g., CDC's) give structure; AI fills in drafts you refine. The judgment about what to measure — and what counts as success — stays yours.

## Practice activities

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Activity 1 · Moderate — Draft a logic model

1. Describe a program (inputs, activities, intended outcomes).
2. Ask AI for a **logic model and 5 candidate indicators**.
3. Check each indicator: is it measurable with data you can get?
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Activity 2 · Moderate — Outline the plan

1. Ask AI for an evaluation plan aligned to the CDC framework steps.
2. Mark which parts need a real data source vs. a placeholder.
:::

## Check your readiness

Answer these, then check — your score suggests whether to dive in or skim the readings first.

<div class="readiness-quiz">
<script type="application/json" class="quiz-data">
{"questions": [{"q": "AI-proposed indicators should be checked for…", "options": ["Being measurable and aligned to goals", "Sounding impressive", "Quantity", "Novelty"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Measurable and aligned beats plausible."}, {"q": "What stays your judgment in evaluation?", "options": ["What to measure and what counts as success", "The font", "The export format", "Nothing"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Defining success is a substantive call."}, {"q": "Frameworks like CDC's provide…", "options": ["Structure that AI drafts can fill in", "A finished evaluation", "A data source", "A funder"], "answer": 0, "explain": "Structure plus AI drafting, refined by you."}]}
</script>
</div>

## Recommended readings

From the AI readings library (ask the facilitator for access):

- **Beyond Vibes: How to Actually Evaluate AI Agents (Simon Wisdom)** — rigor in evaluation thinking (transferable).
- **Making Classifier Evaluation Less Confusing** — clear thinking about metrics.

## Useful resources

- [CDC Program Evaluation Framework](https://www.cdc.gov/evaluation/) — the standard PH evaluation framework.
- [ChatGPT Edu (Drexel AI Tools)](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools) — the approved tool, with data rules.

::: {.callout-warning}
## Data rules
Use **non-sensitive** program details; participant data requires an approved tool. See [Tools & Access](../tools.qmd) and Drexel's [AI Tools page](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools).
:::
